title: Christmas Tree Decor (Pallet Wood or Fence Picket)
summary: A small flat Christmas tree from a single cedar fence picket — three angled boards stacked as narrowing tiers on a trunk block, unlike this catalog's layered stacked-pyramid trees.
description:
Unlike this catalog's layered tree trio, which builds each tree from a multi-piece stacked pyramid of small angled blocks, this tree is just three single boards — cut wider at the bottom, narrower toward the top, each with both ends cut at a matching 30° angle so they read as a simple flat tree silhouette rather than a dimensional stacked form. This is a fast, low-piece-count project, and pallet wood or fence picket scraps work just as well as fresh lumber.

Stack the three angled boards from widest to narrowest, gluing and nailing each to the small 1x3 trunk behind it. The trunk and base pieces anchor the whole assembly and give it a flat back that can lean against a wall or stand freely on a shelf.

Finish with stain or paint — cedar fence picket takes stain well and keeps a rustic, weathered look that fresh pine wouldn't have without extra work.

materials.name: Cedar fence picket, 5-1/2" wide (about half a picket)

steps.title: Cut the tree tiers
steps.body: Cut the three angled tiers, from narrowest at the top to widest at the base.
materials: Cedar fence picket, 5-1/2" wide (about half a picket)

steps.title: Build the trunk and base
steps.body: Attach the base to the bottom of the trunk piece.

steps.title: Attach the tiers
steps.body: Glue and nail the three tiers to the trunk, widest at the bottom, narrowest at the top.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand lightly and apply stain or paint.
